content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff and the changed workflow/final_review/process-backend code. I found 2 fix-now correctness issues in the transient retry implementation. I could not run the Rust test/build commands here because `cargo` is unavailable in this environment.

## Amendments

1. src/adapters/process_backend.rs:61-86; src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:221-225,2587-2593: `is_transient_codex_failure` applies its substring heuristics to any `AppError::InvocationFailed`, regardless of the existing `failure_class`. That means a terminal `DomainValidationFailure` whose validation message happens to mention `503`, `timed out`, `rate limit`, etc. will be retried as transient, and an `InvocationFailed { failure_class: BackendExhausted, details: "Rate limit reached, try again at ..." }` will also burn the transient retry budget before the panel degrades. Both cases violate the bead contract: domain-validation failures must not be retried, and exhausted-backend handling is supposed to stay unchanged.
   Rationale: The retry gate needs to exclude non-transport terminal classes before it falls back to detail-string matching, otherwise final_review will misclassify terminal failures and change stage behavior.
2. src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:289-290 and 2592-2593; src/contexts/workflow_composition/retry_policy.rs:54,63: both retry loops stop at `retry_policy.max_attempts(retry_failure_class)`, so timeout failures only get 3 total attempts. The bead explicitly requires final_review transient retries to run up to the transport retry budget (`max_attempts(FailureClass::TransportFailure)`, currently 5 total invocations). As written, a reviewer/arbiter that times out three times is marked exhausted even though the requested transient retry budget still has two attempts left.
   Rationale: This leaves the single-reviewer case vulnerable to transient timeout failures that should still have been retried under the requested transport budget.
